behavioral_patterns.value:
Network traffic sniffing to packet forgery attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Sniff network traffic to obtain data
  • Step 2: Use obtained data to forge packets
  • Step 3: Make arbitrary requests to target system

Detection: Monitor for unusual network traffic patterns followed by malformed or suspicious packet structures targeting industrial control systems
behavioral_patterns.context: Network traffic sniffing to packet forgery attack chain

behavioral_patterns.value:
PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Enumerate functionality of PLC components
  • Step 2: Reconfigure system settings
  • Step 3: Perform unauthorized file transfers
  • Step 4: Execute remote procedure calls

Detection: Detect sequential enumeration activities followed by configuration changes and file operations on industrial control systems
behavioral_patterns.context: PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

behavioral_patterns.value:
Component discovery to administrative control takeover

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Discover and enumerate PLC components
  • Step 2: Rename system components
  • Step 3: Delete critical files or configurations
  • Step 4: Establish persistent remote access

Detection: Monitor for discovery scans followed by administrative actions like renaming, deletion, and remote access establishment
behavioral_patterns.context: Component discovery to administrative control takeover

snowflake_sql:
-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 3 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Domain: www.ccontrols.com
-- ID: IOC-DOMAIN-6cf26e6d
-- Generation: template (pending)
-- Description: Detects domain indicator 'www.ccontrols.com' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Domain Detection Rule: www.ccontrols.com
-- Source: 1340-1357
-- Type: Domain pattern match (mode: subdomain)
-- Apex Domain: www.ccontrols.com


SELECT
    transaction_id,
    user_id,
    recipient_domain,
    sender_domain,
    transaction_url,
    created_at,
    'www.ccontrols.com' AS matched_indicator,
    '1340-1357' AS source_span
FROM transactions
WHERE (
    recipient_domain LIKE '%www.ccontrols.com%' OR sender_domain LIKE '%www.ccontrols.com%' OR transaction_url LIKE '%www.ccontrols.com%'
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Rule 2: Detect Behavior: PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

B
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-c6f04fa5
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Enumerate functionality of P' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Enumerate functionality of P
-- Source: behavioral_1
-- TTP: T1595
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Enumerate functionality of P' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1595' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_1' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bPLC\ enumeration\ to\ system\ manipulation\ sequence\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Enumerate\ functionality\ of\ P\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bPLC\ enumeration\ to\ system\ manipulation\ sequence\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Enumerate\ functionality\ of\ P\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

-- Rule 3: Detect Behavior: deletion|and remote access establishment
-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-53c3eaf5
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'deletion|and remote access establishment' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: deletion|and remote access establishment
-- Source: behavioral_2
-- TTP: T1082
-- Kill Chain: 
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'deletion|and remote access establishment' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1082' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_2' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bdeletion\|and\ remote\ access\ establishment\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bdeletion\|and\ remote\ access\ establishment\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 4 rules from 4 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: IOC Detection - www.ccontrols.com
// Source: Page 1
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(90d)
| where RemoteUrl has "www.ccontrols.com"
| summarize HitCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, RemoteUrl
| sort by HitCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Network traffic sniffing to packet forgery attack chain
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: 
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence
// Category: discovery
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1595
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where FileName in~ ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| summarize ReconCount = count(),
    UniqueTools = dcount(FileName),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ReconCount >= 3 or UniqueTools >= 2
| sort by ReconCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Component discovery to administrative control takeover
// Category: discovery
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1082
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where FileName in~ ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| summarize ReconCount = count(),
    UniqueTools = dcount(FileName),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where ReconCount >= 3 or UniqueTools >= 2
| sort by ReconCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 4 rules from 4 indicators

``` Rule 1: IOC Detection - www.ccontrols.com
``` Source: Page 1
index=main sourcetype=network "www.ccontrols.com"
| stats count as hit_count, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by src_ip, dest_ip, user
| where hit_count >= 1
| sort -hit_count
| table src_ip, dest_ip, user, hit_count, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - Network traffic sniffing to packet forgery attack chain
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: 
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - PLC enumeration to system manipulation sequence
``` Category: discovery
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1595
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| stats count as recon_count, dc(process_name) as unique_tools, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where recon_count >= 3 OR unique_tools >= 2
| sort -recon_count
| table dest, user, recon_count, unique_tools, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Component discovery to administrative control takeover
``` Category: discovery
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1082
index=main sourcetype=endpoint:process process_name IN ("whoami.exe", "net.exe", "nltest.exe", "ipconfig.exe", "systeminfo.exe")
| stats count as recon_count, dc(process_name) as unique_tools, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by dest, user
| where recon_count >= 3 OR unique_tools >= 2
| sort -recon_count
| table dest, user, recon_count, unique_tools, first_seen, last_seen

# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)

# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - www.ccontrols.com'
id: dac4a070-1d5b-5894-bad3-176e9f9a741e
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
logsource:
    category: dns
    product: any
detection:

        dns.query.name|contains: 'www.ccontrols.com'
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
